title: Investigate whether DiT adds value beyond point prediction

exp0_dynamic_subset.description: Per-quartile analysis of DA8 models by copy-baseline difficulty
exp0_dynamic_subset.finding: DiT-x0 is behind MLP-residual on ALL quartiles including Q1 (hardest). The gap does NOT shrink on hard subsets. DiT underperforms even more on easier quartiles where copy baseline is strong.

exp1_diversity.description: K=10 DDIM diversity diagnostic with eta=0 (varying x_T)
exp1_diversity.finding: DiT-x0 has learned a near-unimodal conditional posterior. Pairwise distance across K=10 samples is very small (0.002-0.053), confirming the model functions as a point predictor. vjepa2_rep64 shows near-zero diversity (0.002). vit_s16 shows modest diversity (0.053) where Best-of-K oracle exceeds MLP-residual, but Mean-of-K (the accessible metric) does not.

vit_s16.note: Best-of-K is oracle; Mean-of-K is accessible. Oracle improves over MLP-residual but accessible metric does not.

vjepa2_rep64.note: Near-complete mode collapse. DiT ignores noise prior entirely.

exp2_rollout.description: Autoregressive long rollout (dropped from scope per plan)

exp3_horizon.description: DiT-x0 vs MLP at longer horizons (h=8, h=16)
exp3_horizon.finding: DiT-x0 falls further behind MLP-residual at longer horizons. The gap WIDENS from h=4 to h=8/h=16 on every encoder. The hypothesis that DiT closes the gap when prediction becomes harder is REJECTED.

gate.result: 5/6 encoders pass absolute proximity at h=16, but gap widens vs h=4
gate.note: Negative values = MLP-residual wins. Gap sign: positive = DiT wins.

overall_finding: DiT-x0 provides no measurable advantage over MLP-residual in any tested setting: not on hard subsets (Exp 0), not at longer horizons (Exp 3), and it has collapsed to a near-point-predictor with minimal sampling diversity (Exp 1). At 2Hz nuScenes, the conditional future distribution is effectively unimodal, so diffusion adds computational overhead without representational benefit. This is a clean negative result that constrains when diffusion world models add value for autonomous driving.
caveats: Best-of-K is an oracle metric -- real systems cannot select the best sample without ground truth
2Hz nuScenes may be too slow to capture multi-modal futures (e.g. lane change decisions happen within 0.5s)
MLP-residual is a stronger baseline than MLP-fair; papers comparing only against weaker baselines overstate diffusion value
Negative results constrain when diffusion adds value, which is itself a contribution
